sources.name: Federal Highway Administration — National Scenic Byways

paragraphs: The trail was named a National Scenic Byway on June 15, 2000. It is a West Virginia State Scenic Byway as well.

references.text: Staff. "Washington Heritage Trail". National Scenic Byways. Federal Highway Administration. Retrieved September 11, 2011.
Staff. "Official Designations". Washington Heritage Trail. Federal Highway Administration. Retrieved September 11, 2011.

gallery.cap: National Scenic Byway Washington Heritage (West Virginia)

gallery.desc: A stately home along the Washington Heritage Trail reveals part of the history of the area. Location: West Virginia Status: Public domain.
Visit the country's first spa at Berkeley Springs. Location: Berkeley Springs, West Virginia Status: Public domain. Photo by Dennis Adams

Trees full of pink flowers in spring contrast with the solid white pillars of the gazebo in Berkeley Springs State Park. Location: Berkeley Springs State Park, Berkeley Springs, West Virginia Status: Public domain. Photo by Jeanne Mozier

gallery.cap: Enjoying a Break in Washington's Bathtub
gallery.desc: A hat-wearing man perches on the side of George Washington's Bathtub, putting his feet in the water at Berkeley Springs State Park. Location: Berkeley Springs, West Virginia Status: Public domain. Photo Courtesy of Travel Berkeley Springs

gallery.cap: Female Participants of a Shepherdstown Living History Event
gallery.desc: Dressed in historical dress, two women and a young girl holding a rag doll pose before a background showing signs of autumn. Location: Shepherdstown, West Virginia Status: Public domain. Photo Courtesy of Travel Berkeley Springs

gallery.cap: Free Spring Water at Berkeley Springs
gallery.desc: A woman fills a container with free spring water from a stone dispenser at Berkeley Springs State Park. Location: Berkeley Springs State Park, West Virginia Status: Public domain. Photo by Steve Shaluta, Courtesy Travel Berkeley Springs
A Berkeley Springs visitor stops by what might have been a bathtub used by General Washington himself. Location: Berkeley Springs, West Virginia Status: Public domain. Photo by Dennis Adams

gallery.cap: George Washington's Bathtub in Berkeley Springs State Park
gallery.desc: A spring filled cutaway in the rock at Berkeley Springs State Park is known as "George Washington's Bathtub." Location: Berkeley Springs State Park, Berkeley Springs, West Virginia Status: Public domain. Photo by Jeanne Mozier

gallery.cap: Hazy Blue Skies Near Mills Gap
gallery.desc: Hayfields dot the extensive green landscape while tree-covered hills slope gently in the distance. Status: Public domain.
Visitors stand around and chat about the hot springs near one of the bathhouses at Berkeley Springs. Location: Berkeley Springs, West Virginia Status: Public domain. Photo by Dennis Adams of FHWA

gallery.cap: Kids Finding a Dip in George Washington's Bathtub Irresistible
gallery.desc: Two children smile from the rectangular "bathtub" at Berkeley Springs in the summer time. Location: Berkeley Springs, West Virginia Status: Public domain. Photo Courtesy of Travel Berkeley Springs
